method: 30-minute remote interviews + post-interview structured pricing survey
field: Conducted Jan 2026 across IIM-K + IIM-L + ISB + Razorpay + Cred + 4 founder-led Series-A startups

respondents.name: Ananya R.
respondents.quote: I'd pay ₹199 in a heartbeat. ₹299 makes me think — but only if Defence Interview is included. ₹499 feels like a course, not an app.

respondents.name: Vikram J.
respondents.quote: I'm cynical about subscriptions. Show me the Defence Interview output once, free, then I'll commit. Otherwise ₹0.

respondents.name: Priya M.
respondents.quote: I don't care about ₹49,999 per hire if it actually filters to 5 real candidates. Just don't bill me upfront.

respondents.name: Rahul K.
respondents.quote: ₹299 is fine. ₹499 only if Career Path becomes a recurring thing I check monthly, not a one-shot tool.

respondents.name: Sahana G.
respondents.quote: If it gets me past resume filters at Flipkart, I'd pay ₹500. If it's just a fancy LinkedIn, ₹99.

respondents.name: Aditya S.
respondents.quote: I'd use the ATS scanner once a month. Not worth more than ₹150 just for that. Need bundled value.

respondents.name: Trisha B.
respondents.quote: Career Path scan is the magic. I'd pay ₹299 just for that, monthly.

respondents.name: Karan P.
respondents.quote: If credentials are W3C-portable to US employers, ₹699 makes sense. Otherwise ₹99 if anything.

respondents.name: Madhuri Y.
respondents.quote: I'm the buyer for the recruiter side. ₹50k/hire is non-controversial. ₹25k/challenge upfront isn't, even if I trust the AI ranking.

respondents.name: Saurabh M.
respondents.quote: I'd pay ₹399 if Defence Interview is unlimited and bundled. Anything less, ₹199.

respondents.name: Diya V.
respondents.quote: Annual ₹2,499 is a steal for what I see. Monthly ₹299 feels expensive — psychology, I know.

respondents.name: Arjun N.
respondents.quote: I'll pay 8% of CTC if you find someone real. Won't pay ₹25k upfront just to post.

respondents.name: Komal D.
respondents.quote: ₹99 max. Anything more, I'd ask my dad and feel guilty.

respondents.name: Anand R.
respondents.quote: Career Path Engine alone is worth ₹299. The rest is a bonus.

respondents.name: Naina M.
respondents.quote: If Defence Interview is the AI mock-interview thing, I'd pay ₹199 happily. The brand world doesn't talk about credentials yet though.

respondents.name: Pranav K.
respondents.quote: I'd pay ₹499 if it's a one-time mint. Subscription model feels weird for credentials.

respondents.name: Riya K.
respondents.quote: I'd pay ₹149 if there's a parents-can-see-this resume report. ₹299 only if it gets me a referral.

respondents.name: Vignesh R.
respondents.quote: Defence Interview is the killer feature. ₹399 for that bundled, fine.

respondents.name: Tejas P.
respondents.quote: Worth ₹699 if it's W3C-portable and recognized abroad. Otherwise ₹99-199 only.

respondents.name: Anvesh G.
respondents.quote: ₹199 is the sweet spot. I'll cancel after I get my MBA admit.

respondents.name: Bhavin S.
respondents.quote: I'd pay ₹499 to retrain. Brand sales doesn't open PM doors easily here.

respondents.name: Subhankar D.
respondents.quote: ₹249 OK if Defence Interview is unlimited. I run mock interviews twice a week.

respondents.name: Kavya B.
respondents.quote: I want yearly billing. ₹2,499 fine. Monthly hurts my budgeting brain.

respondents.name: Mohit S.
respondents.quote: ₹399 with credentials + Defence + Career Path bundled. Anything stripped down, ₹149.

respondents.name: Heer P.
respondents.quote: Honestly, my placement cell does all this. I'd pay ₹99 just for the W3C credential, as a hedge.

respondents.name: Akhila T.
respondents.quote: If credentials open Andela/Mercor doors without their cut, ₹699 instantly.

respondents.name: Souvik B.
respondents.quote: ₹299 fine. Top-of-feed badge worth ₹50 extra to me but not ₹200.

respondents.name: Sneha K.
respondents.quote: ₹399 with bundled Defence Interview and Career Path is fair value. I expense it on my dev fund anyway.

aggregated.method: Stated max WTP per respondent + clustering by preferred price band

outliers.high: 5 respondents at ₹699 conditional on global W3C verifier value

aggregated.annualPreference: 11/28 said they'd switch to annual billing if discount >= 30%
aggregated.outcomePricingForRecruiters: Universal preference — 4/4 recruiters interviewed said no upfront fee

validation_summary.agreement: Strong directional match (±₹50). LLM slightly overshoots due to feature-maximizer skew; live research shows higher price-sensitivity at lower-income segments.
deltas_to_address: Live respondents emphasize annual billing more than LLMs did — bump annual discount to 35-40%
Defence Interview is more pivotal in real conversations than LLM responses suggested — consider including it in base Pro tier
Tier-2/3 candidates value parents-can-see resume report — add to roadmap
